name: Town Crier
description: A stout man in a feathered cap, clutching a brass bell and a scroll of announcements.
backstory:
Cedric has been Northkeep's town crier for twenty-three years. Before that, he was a travelling messenger for the Crown Post, and he still carries himself with the pride of someone who once delivered dispatches to lords.

He knows every rumour, scandal, and shipping manifest in Northkeep — much more than he lets on. He has a particular fondness for gossip and dramatic flair, and he never misses an opportunity to ring his bell.

He is deeply loyal to Northkeep and its people. He worries about the strange lights from the Eltharan ruins to the north and the smuggler activity in the cove, but he lacks the courage to investigate himself. He admires anyone brave enough to look into these threats.

He dislikes the harbour master, whom he considers corrupt, and is on warm terms with Priestess Sera and Gorath. He knows Old Nell at the lighthouse has secrets but respects her privacy. He has never entered the ruins and is frankly terrified of them.

start.text: Hear ye, hear ye! Welcome to Northkeep, traveller! What news do you seek?
start.narrative: The town crier straightens his cap and peers at you expectantly.

news.text: Strange lights have been seen near the old ruins to the north! And the docks have been busy — rumour has it smugglers are using the cove east of the lighthouse.
news.narrative: He leans in and lowers his voice.

directions.text: The market lies to the east — fine goods and Gorath's forge. South leads to the docks and harbour. The Iron Gate stands to the north, and beyond it the open road. And the Temple of the Silver Moon? Just west of here.
directions.narrative: He gestures grandly in each direction.

ruins.text: Old Eltharan ruins, they are. Been sealed for centuries. But folk say the ground shakes at night now, and there's a glow from the catacombs beneath. The priestess at the temple knows more, I'd wager.

smugglers.text: Old Nell at the lighthouse sees everything, but she keeps her secrets. The harbour master turns a blind eye — or so they say. If you're brave, the cove is east of the lighthouse. But mind you don't get caught.
smugglers.narrative: He taps the side of his nose.

responses.text: Tell me about the ruins instead.
